summary: Verified the managed CLI/global runtime harness now serializes managed-process tests with the shared runtime lock. The focused ports lane passes, and the combined managed/global runtime command fails only on the intended T012/T013 RED behavior assertions.

validations.summary: Focused ports test passed: 1 passed, 0 failed.
Combined run passed the ports lane and failed 10 intended RED assertions: 23 passed, 10 failed, 0 unexpected harness failures.

expected_red_failures: test/global-runtime.test.js: start project:task writes a global run index record with lifecycle identity
test/global-runtime.test.js: start project:task injects global run markers into the managed task environment
test/global-runtime.test.js: start project:task returns the existing run without spawning a second process
test/global-runtime.test.js: start project:task reports HTTP readiness separately from process liveness
test/global-runtime.test.js: start project:task falls back to process-alive readiness when no port is declared
test/global-runtime.test.js: status --all exposes safe next actions for active owned runs
test/global-runtime.test.js: project remove refuses to unregister a project with active owned runs
test/managed-cli.test.js: start --json returns run identity and readiness fields for the managed run
test/managed-cli.test.js: start --json injects launchdeck run markers into the managed task environment
test/managed-cli.test.js: start --json treats a second owned start as idempotent without spawning another process
